questions.original_question: What was Zeekr’s employee headcount and functional distribution as of June 30, 2023?
questions.answer:
As of June 30, 2023, Zeekr’s employee headcount is not explicitly detailed in the available information. However, it is noted that as of December 31, 2023, CEVT, a subsidiary of Zeekr Technology, employed 715 individuals, which may provide some context regarding Zeekr’s workforce size.

In terms of functional distribution, Zeekr demonstrated a strong emphasis on research and development, with expenses totaling RMB 1,383,501 thousand. The company also incurred significant selling, general, and administrative expenses amounting to RMB 1,614,305 thousand, reflecting its operational and marketing efforts. Other operating income, net, was RMB 76,488 thousand, indicating additional revenue streams. Total operating expenses stood at RMB 2,921,318 thousand, while the loss from operations was RMB 1,370,992 thousand. Interest expense was RMB 87,364 thousand, and interest income was RMB 18,512 thousand. Other income (expenses), net, recorded RMB 27,040 thousand. The loss before income tax expense and share of losses in equity method investments was RMB 1,412,804 thousand. These figures underscore Zeekr’s financial activities and operational focus during that period.

This information is based on data available before 2024-04-25 and 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021, and revenues from vehicle sales increased on a quarterly basis in 2022, driven by the volume of vehicles delivered. Revenues from vehicle sales temporarily decreased in the first quarter of 2023 amid market uncertainties and fluctuations surrounding economic recovery in China, and due to the declined deliveries as a result of the temporary suspension of the ZEEKR Factory for production line upgrades in January and February 2023, but quickly returned to normal levels in the remaining three quarters of 2023. Revenues from sales of batteries and other components were on a relatively upward trend on a quarterly basis in 2022 and the six months ended June 30, 2023, which began to increase significantly in the three months ended June 30, 2022. This was attributed to the increased sales volume to related parties within Geely Group. Revenues from sales of batteries and other components decreased in the third quarter of 2023 due to less demand from related parties within Geely Group with the shift of their product mix in the same quarter, and increased in the fourth quarter of 2023 in line with the increased sales of battery packs. Revenues from research and development services and other services were also on a relatively upward trend on a quarterly basis in 2022, and decreased significantly in the first quarter of 2023, attributable to fewer R&D services provided to related parties compared with the last quarter of 2022.

metadata.title_summary:
title: CEVT 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB, also known as CEVT, is a Swedish research and development center established in 2013, focusing on automotive innovation for international markets, particularly in Europe and the United States. CEVT specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and passenger battery electric vehicles (BEVs) that comply with local standards. The center is also developing advanced digital tools such as data analytics, smart cockpits, and fleet operations, while emphasizing user-centric systems that integrate in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities. 

One of CEVT's key projects is the SEA-M platform, an enhanced version of its SEA platform designed to support ZEEKR's future BEVs. This platform will feature autonomous driving capabilities and smart mobility options, promising a superior driving experience with innovative designs like a B-pillar-less structure and advanced air conditioning technologies. As of December 31, 2023, CEVT employed 715 individuals, primarily focused on mobility technologies and product development.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Zeekr Tech EU 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB (Zeekr Tech EU), established in 2013 in Sweden, serves as a research and development hub focused on creating innovative products for international markets. The organization specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and battery electric vehicles (BEVs) tailored to local standards, alongside digital offerings like data analytics and smart cockpit functions. A significant focus is placed on advancing BEV technologies that prioritize user-centric systems, integrating in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities with centralized architecture for improved user experiences.

Central to Zeekr Tech EU's initiatives is the SEA-M platform, an evolution of the SEA architecture designed to support autonomous driving and smart mobility. This platform aims to deliver versatile mobility options, exceptional driving experiences, and long-lasting vehicles that can be continuously upgraded. BEVs developed on this platform, such as the Zeekr Mix and Waymo RT robotaxi, feature innovative designs, including wide door openings and flexible interiors. By the end of 2024, the company employed 733 individuals, focusing on mobility technologies, digital solutions, and future product development.

title: 7. Inventories 
summary: The inventory summary for Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, as of December 31 for the years 2021, 2022, and 2023, reveals significant trends in inventory composition over this three-year period. The inventory is categorized into raw materials, work in progress, and finished products, with values expressed in thousands of RMB.

From 2021 to 2023, raw materials inventory showed notable fluctuations, starting at 375,837 thousand RMB in 2021, peaking at 1,129,060 thousand RMB in 2022, and then declining to 733,069 thousand RMB in 2023. Work in progress inventory increased from 34,785 thousand RMB in 2021 to 48,216 thousand RMB in 2022, before slightly decreasing to 40,142 thousand RMB in 2023. In contrast, finished products experienced remarkable growth, rising from 803,458 thousand RMB in 2021 to 1,987,533 thousand RMB in 2022, and further escalating to 4,455,479 thousand RMB in 2023.

Overall, the total inventory value exhibited substantial growth, increasing from 1,214,080 thousand RMB in 2021 to 3,164,809 thousand RMB in 2022, and reaching 5,228,689 thousand RMB in 2023. This upward trend underscores a robust increase in inventory value across the three years. Additionally, the report includes information on the allowance for doubtful accounts related to accounts receivable, as well as a summary of prepayments and other current assets, providing a comprehensive overview of the company's financial standing.

title: Selected Quarterly Results of Operations 
summary: The table provides a detailed overview of the unaudited quarterly financial performance of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited from March 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. This summary should be considered alongside the company's consolidated financial statements and related notes for a comprehensive understanding of its financial health.

During the observed periods, ZEEKR demonstrated significant growth in vehicle sales, particularly in the latter half of 2023, where Q4 revenues peaked at RMB 16,357,925, driven by a record vehicle sales revenue of RMB 10,592,647. Despite fluctuations in total costs, which reached RMB (14,029,598) in Q4 2023, gross profit showed an upward trend, culminating at RMB 2,328,327 in the same quarter. 

The company faced challenges in early 2023, with a decline in revenues to RMB 8,620,408 in Q1 due to market uncertainties and production halts. However, revenues rebounded in subsequent quarters, reflecting a recovery in production and sales. Battery and component sales also exhibited growth, although demand fluctuated, particularly in Q3 2023, before recovering in Q4.

Research and development (R&D) revenues followed a similar pattern, initially declining in early 2023 but increasing again in the latter half of the year due to enhanced service offerings and out-licensed technology revenues. 

Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, rose significantly from Q4 2022 to Q4 2023, driven by increased marketing efforts related to new model launches and higher R&D expenses linked to ongoing projects. Although the company experienced increasing losses from operations throughout the observed periods, the overall financial trajectory indicates a robust recovery and growth potential, particularly in vehicle sales.

In conclusion, ZEEKR's financial results illustrate a dynamic and evolving business landscape, marked by recovery from earlier setbacks and a strong focus on expanding its product offerings and operational capabilities. The management's adjustments in financial reporting reflect a commitment to accurately portraying the company's performance in a stable operational environment.

title: 2021 Incentive Plan 
summary: In August 2021, the Group launched the "2021 Plan," an incentive program allowing for the issuance of up to 150 million ordinary shares in equity-based awards aimed at benefiting management, employees, and other eligible individuals. The plan involves awarding Restricted Share Units (RSUs) from 2021 to 2024, which are subject to a four-year service vesting requirement and performance conditions linked to the Group's operational metrics, including automobile sales volume, market share, and EBITDA. A key stipulation of the plan is the IPO Condition, requiring the Company’s ordinary shares to be listed as securities for RSU vesting.

Since the plan's inception, the Group has issued multiple grants of RSUs. As of September 30, 2022, a total of 32,472,920 RSUs were awarded to management and employees, with an additional 599,836 RSUs for employees of equity method investees, and 4,884,400 RSUs for specific personnel within Geely Auto, Geely Holding, and related companies. By June 30, 2023, the Group granted 16,599,500 RSUs to its management and employees, along with 60,000 RSUs for equity method investees' employees and 1,295,800 RSUs for select Geely personnel. On April 10, 2024, further grants included 29,519,364 RSUs for management and employees, 211,444 RSUs for equity method investees, and 2,041,000 RSUs for specific Geely staff. These allocations underscore the Group's commitment to incentivizing and rewarding its workforce and associated entities.

title: ZEEKR X 
summary: In April 2023, ZEEKR introduced the ZEEKR X, a compact SUV distinguished by its innovative interior design, cutting-edge technology, and exceptional driving performance. Following its launch, deliveries began in June 2023, contributing RMB 4,174.2 million (approximately US$ 587.9 million) in revenue by the end of the year, representing 8.1% of the company's total annual revenue. 

The ZEEKR X is designed to enhance the driving experience through its advanced features, including a spacious layout with dimensions of 4,450 mm in length, 1,836 mm in width, and a height of 1,572 mm, along with a wheelbase of 2,750 mm. It is powered by a 66 kWh battery, providing a maximum CLTC range of 512 km and an all-wheel drive system with dual electric motors, achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 3.7 seconds and delivering a peak torque of 543 N·m.

Key technological innovations include a frameless door design, an augmented reality head-up display, a movable central display, and a premium Yamaha audio system. The vehicle also features advanced intelligence capabilities such as Face ID bio-recognition, a smart assistant named AI Mate, and autonomous driving technology branded as ZEEKR AD. 

Notably, the ZEEKR X offers a unique four-seat model that enhances rear passenger comfort and allows for easy seat folding via dashboard controls for additional cargo space. An interactive B-pillar with a 3D camera and touchscreen enables passengers to manage vehicle access and status from outside. Overall, the ZEEKR X stands out in the SUV market with its blend of performance, comfort, and modern technology, reflecting the brand's commitment to sustainability and efficiency.

summary: Loss per share (LPS) is a key financial metric that reflects the loss attributable to each outstanding share of a company's stock, calculated by dividing the net loss by the total number of shares. This metric is vital for investors as it provides insight into a company's financial performance on a per-share basis, with higher LPS indicating greater losses that can adversely affect investor sentiment and stock valuation. 

Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, has reported its LPS figures from its inception. The company's net loss from consolidated entities increased significantly from approximately $2.3 billion in 2021 to around $7.7 billion in 2022, before slightly rising to about $8.3 billion in 2023. In contrast, its subsidiary Ningbo Viridi transitioned from a net loss of approximately $151 million in 2021 to a net income of $278 million in 2022, maintaining a positive net income of about $82 million in 2023. 

The net loss attributable to ordinary shareholders rose sharply from approximately $2.1 billion in 2021 to nearly $7.9 billion in 2022, and further to about $8.3 billion in 2023. The weighted average number of ordinary shares outstanding increased from approximately 1.5 billion in 2021 to 2 billion in both 2022 and 2023, leading to a basic net loss per share that escalated from $(1.43) in 2021 to $(3.97) in 2022, and $(4.17) in 2023.

The calculations for diluted net loss per share excluded certain restricted share units and convertible preferred shares due to their anti-dilutive effects. The types of shares involved include restricted share units, Series Pre-A preferred shares, and Series A preferred shares, each representing different stages of equity ownership and often used to incentivize employees and attract investors.

A detailed overview of share counts relevant for dilution calculations shows that the number of restricted share units and Series Pre-A preferred shares increased from 2021 to 2023, reflecting the evolving equity structure of Zeekr. summary: The company is strategically dedicated to the design, engineering, development, and sale of premium battery electric vehicles (BEVs), emphasizing advanced technology, drivability, and user experience. With strong research and development capabilities and synergies from the Geely Group, it aims to leverage the burgeoning premium BEV market in China, projected to grow significantly from 666,400 units in 2024 to 2,607,600 units by 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan.

As of December 2023, ZEEKR has delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, predominantly in China, reflecting a rapid growth trajectory in the premium BEV sector.

In terms of global expansion, ZEEKR began delivering vehicles in Europe in December 2023 and plans to supply the Waymo One Fleet in the U.S. The monthly delivery data for 2023 and early 2024 showcases a strong upward trend, with significant milestones such as 16,089 deliveries in April 2024. This consistent growth illustrates ZEEKR's successful penetration into the premium electric vehicle market and its increasing consumer acceptance, particularly for the ZEEKR 001 model. Overall, ZEEKR is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for premium BEVs both in China and internationally.

title: CEVT 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB, also known as CEVT, is a Swedish research and development center established in 2013, focusing on automotive innovation for international markets, particularly in Europe and the United States. CEVT specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and passenger battery electric vehicles (BEVs) that comply with local standards. The center is also developing advanced digital tools such as data analytics, smart cockpits, and fleet operations, while emphasizing user-centric systems that integrate in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities. 

One of CEVT's key projects is the SEA-M platform, an enhanced version of its SEA platform designed to support ZEEKR's future BEVs. This platform will feature autonomous driving capabilities and smart mobility options, promising a superior driving experience with innovative designs like a B-pillar-less structure and advanced air conditioning technologies. As of December 31, 2023, CEVT employed 715 individuals, primarily focused on mobility technologies and product development.

summary: ZEEKR is strategically positioned in the premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, focusing on the design, engineering, and sales of advanced electric vehicles that enhance user experience and drivability. Share-based compensation and depreciation expenses also rose, underscoring the financial strain. The adjustments in accounts receivable and inventory further illustrated the challenges faced by Zeekr in managing its financial health.

Overall, the combined financial statements provide a comprehensive view of Zeekr's equity structure, financial performance, and cash flow dynamics, emphasizing the impact of various transactions and losses on the company's fiscal standing.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OUR VEHICLES 
summary: ZEEKR is strategically positioned in the premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, focusing on the design, engineering, and sales of advanced electric vehicles that enhance user experience and drivability. Leveraging its strong research and development capabilities and synergies with the Geely Group, ZEEKR aims to tap into the rapidly growing premium BEV market in China, which is projected to see sales increase from 666,400 units in 2024 to 2,607,600 units by 2028, reflecting a compound annual growth rate of 40.6%. 

Since its launch of the ZEEKR 001 in 2021, the company has expanded its lineup with models like the ZEEKR 009 and ZEEKR X, while also planning to introduce an upscale sedan. As of December 31, 2023, ZEEKR has delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, showcasing one of the fastest growth rates in the premium BEV segment. The company is now also entering the European market and has plans for future deliveries in the United States.

In terms of delivery performance, ZEEKR has consistently exceeded 10,000 units per month since mid-2023, with notable peaks such as 13,476 units in December 2023. This impressive growth trajectory underscores ZEEKR's commitment to expanding its market presence and catering to diverse customer preferences in the evolving landscape of next-generation mobility.

summary: The table provides a detailed overview of the unaudited quarterly financial performance of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited from March 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. This summary should be considered alongside the company's consolidated financial statements and related notes for a comprehensive understanding of its financial health.

During the observed periods, ZEEKR demonstrated significant growth in vehicle sales, particularly in the latter half of 2023, where Q4 revenues peaked at RMB 16,357,925, driven by a record vehicle sales revenue of RMB 10,592,647. Despite fluctuations in total costs, which reached RMB (14,029,598) in Q4 2023, gross profit showed an upward trend, culminating at RMB 2,328,327 in the same quarter. 

The company faced challenges in early 2023, with a decline in revenues to RMB 8,620,408 in Q1 due to market uncertainties and production halts. However, revenues rebounded in subsequent quarters, reflecting a recovery in production and sales. Battery and component sales also exhibited growth, although demand fluctuated, particularly in Q3 2023, before recovering in Q4.

Research and development (R&D) revenues followed a similar pattern, initially declining in early 2023 but increasing again in the latter half of the year due to enhanced service offerings and out-licensed technology revenues. 

Operating expenses, including selling, general, and administrative costs, rose significantly from Q4 2022 to Q4 2023, driven by increased marketing efforts related to new model launches and higher R&D expenses linked to ongoing projects. Although the company experienced increasing losses from operations throughout the observed periods, the overall financial trajectory indicates a robust recovery and growth potential, particularly in vehicle sales.

In conclusion, ZEEKR's financial results illustrate a dynamic and evolving business landscape, marked by recovery from earlier setbacks and a strong focus on expanding its product offerings and operational capabilities. The management's adjustments in financial reporting reflect a commitment to accurately portraying the company's performance in a stable operational environment.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology offers full lifecycle updates to its battery electric vehicles (BEVs) through firmware over-the-air (FOTA) updates to keep enhancing drivability, functionality, and user experience under multiple driving scenarios. This allows Lotus Technology's vehicles to keep pace with the latest market developments, evolving technological advancements, and customer preferences, thus constantly bringing improved experiences to its customers. Within 100 days since the vehicle delivery, Lotus Technology has completed two major FOTA updates with over 30 new features added for the ZEEKR 001. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had released 10 major rounds of FOTA updates to the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 009, and ZEEKR X, which optimized the door control, seat adjustment, facial recognition, driving range improvement, and air conditioning functions, among others.

metadata.title_summary:
title: How will you receive dividends and other distributions on the shares? 
summary: The depositary is responsible for distributing cash dividends and other distributions from shares or deposited securities to holders of American Depositary Shares (ADS), after deducting its fees and applicable taxes. Distributions are based on the number of shares represented by the ADSs held, and cash dividends may be converted into U.S. dollars if feasible; otherwise, foreign currency will be distributed to eligible holders. Any unconverted currency will be held without interest, and fractional cents will be rounded to the nearest whole cent. 

In cases where shares are issued as dividends, the depositary may distribute additional ADSs, sell fractional shares, or retain existing ADSs to represent new shares. The depositary also manages rights to purchase additional shares by either exercising the rights on behalf of ADS holders, distributing the rights, or selling them and sharing the proceeds after fees. If no action is taken, the rights will lapse.

For other distributed items related to deposited securities, the depositary may either sell them and distribute the proceeds or retain them, making the ADSs representative of the new property. However, it will only distribute non-ADS securities if it receives legal confirmation from the issuer. The depositary disclaims responsibility for distributions deemed unlawful or impractical and is not obligated to register ADSs or related securities under U.S. law, which may limit the distribution of certain securities to ADS holders. Thus, holders may not receive distributions if it is illegal or impractical for the depositary to provide them.
